verified: exact route-reference milepoints with route identity, signed direction, and jurisdiction

a US 36 numbering progression and an I-25 jurisdiction reset
deterministic omission when an exact route-reference anchor is absent

failure_logs.failure: Six hosted recovery runs exposed official-engine cold-start route-context work inside the unchanged 50-millisecond visual chunk budget.
failure_logs.recovery: Cached one deterministic route-context plan per edge before visual timing while retaining route sampling and all generated geometry inside the budget.

failure_logs.failure: CodeRabbit reached its temporary review limit before reviewing the final recovery commits.
failure_logs.recovery: Completed the repository-mandated independent adversarial review; substantive findings were fixed and the full protected checks remain required before merge.

recovery_result: Local full gate, route-context profile, and visibility-asserting capture passed; protected Linux and Windows checks passed; the project owner approved current-milestone high-speed readability on 2026-07-18.

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: mile markers use exact route-reference values instead of trip progress
acceptance_comparisons.observed: US 36 mile 42, US 287 mile 250, US 36 mile 43, and I-25 mile 214 were independently preserved; the requested marker-us36-missing-anchor record produced a provenance-bearing omission and no synthetic marker

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: numbering resets and route concurrency remain deterministic and non-contradictory
acceptance_comparisons.observed: two concurrent eastbound identities were placed on opposite shoulders and the receiving southbound I-25 marker reset to jurisdiction CO-I25 mile 214

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: exit and transfer signs expose complete semantic context
acceptance_comparisons.observed: exit 42A displays US 36 and CO 93 shields, Boulder Junction, Nederland, lane 2, fuel, and food; exit 44 displays US 36 and I-25 South shields, Denver, and right-lane guidance

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: placement is deterministic, separated, inside the declared preview envelope, and streamed within the visual build budget
acceptance_comparisons.observed: guide signs maintain at least 60 meters separation; each current-point label asserted visible tree state, camera-frustum inclusion, positive forward distance, and declared-range compliance from up to 102 meters; the run remained under the 50-millisecond initial build budget with zero chunk failures

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: sign text is realistic and legible at high-speed approach distances
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the deterministic visibility envelope and five-frame contact sheet are complete; the project owner approved current graybox readability on 2026-07-18 while retaining production signage realism and legibility as P1-009 scope

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: automated captures cover every required route-context case
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the five-frame contact sheet covers concurrent markers, exit-only guidance, an ordinary marker, a highway transfer, and a numbering reset
